\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
I don't need any food right now.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Can you give me two hotdogs, please?

\c[2]Food Vendor:\c[0]
Sure. There you go.
MessageRows 1
\>You got two \c[13]hotdogs\c[0]!

\c[2]Food Vendor:\c[0]
That will be 10 bucks.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Here is your ten bucks.
Quest 8 Change Description Entry To 3
Quest 8 Complete Objective 2
Quest 8 Show Objective 3
\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
I don't need to buy anything else.
\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
I don't need any food right now.

\c[2]Man:\c[0]
Excuse me, can I talk to you for a minute?

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Sorry, I'm not in the mood.

\c[2]Man:\c[0]
Are you sure? Because I think you need to listen to what I have to say.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
And why is that?
(\c[23]She said, getting more annoyed by the second.\c[0])

\c[2]Man:\c[0]
Because I think I have a solution to your problem.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
What are you talking about?

\c[2]Man:\c[0]
You know, the big problem you are having with your old employers.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
How do you know about that?

\c[2]Man:\c[0]
Well, let me first introduce myself.

\c[2]Man:\c[0]
My name is Tomas. I'm also an ex-employee of GRN.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Really! Were you also fired from the company?

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Yep, I was.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
What for?

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Well, I was trying to follow in your footsteps.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
I discovered what you tried to do and wanted to finish your job.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
But unlike you, I went to the journalist instead of the cops.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Although, it turned out the journalist I went to was in their pocket too.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
He went straight to my boss, and they kicked me out of the building immediately.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Couldn't you just leak those documents on the internet anyway?

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
I could have. If I actually had those data in hand.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
But I only had access to them. I thought I should first contact a journalist before making a copy.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Yeah, I know, it was a big mistake.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
So you are the reason they are coming after me. So that no one in the future dares to do something like that.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Yeah, sorry about that.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
But that's why I'm here, to help you.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
How do you know I will be here anyway?

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
I went to your house to look for you.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Your mom said you came to talk with your former boss.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
So here I am.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Quite the deductive work you have done.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Yeah. So anyway, How did it go with your ex-boss?

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Just as I expected. He threatened to make my life a living hell. Well, more hell than it already is.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
But you do know, he can't win that lawsuit?

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Corporate espionage is not illegal. And all they have is your video of stealing some files...

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Yeah, yeah. I know all of this. I've done my research.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
But it won't stop him from coming after me.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Mr. Albert, I mean, the CEO has a personal vendetta against me.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
He has influence over all the smaller businesses of the city. So no one wants to be on his bad side by hiring me.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Or you can leave town. This is what I'm gonna do.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
I don't know. I grew up here. My friends, family, and everyone is here.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
But I guess this is an option I need to consider.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
There might be another way to turn your life around and even get some revenge against your former employers.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Really! How?

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Before I go into further details, Let me ask you one question.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
How far are you willing to go to get back at them?

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Well, one thing I learned from the last few months is, You can't always win by playing fair.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Especially if you are going after a corrupt corporation like GRN.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
So yes, I am willing to go as far as I need to go, as long as it's not hurting me or affecting me in any way.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
That's up to you. Depends on how you decide to play the game.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Then let's hear it. Tell me what I need to do?

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
You need to meet someone.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
His name is Victor, and he is a mob boss.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
What! I did not expect that!

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
I did ask you how far you are willing to go.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Yes, but working with a criminal...

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
He is not a common criminal. In fact, he is a very decent guy. As long as you don't cross him in any way.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Think about it. What else can you lose?

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
I could go to jail for a long time.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
You don't even know what kind of work he would want you to do.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
I can arrange a meeting with him.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
He is a very powerful man. Maybe the only one powerful enough to take down GRN.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Don't worry. As I said, he is a decent man. As long as you are not on his bad side.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
And another reason I think He might help you is he has his own beef with big corporations like GRN.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Because you see, he had a very humble beginning.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
He grew up in a slum, watching people having a hard time living the life when big executives of those...

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
...companies were taking advantage of people's misery to make more money than they could count.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
So if you can convince him of all the wrongdoing of GRN, he will probably be more than eager to help you.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Yeah, but I don't have any evidence of their wrongdoing.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Well, it's a good thing he is not a judge. You just have to be very convincing.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
I guess I can give it a shot. And if he wants me to do something I'm uncomfortable with, I can always back down, right?

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Yes, of course.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
So, where can I meet him?

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
His "office" is in the slum area of the city. It's the biggest building in that area.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
In the slum! I never went there!

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Will you go with me?

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Sorry, I can't. I am leaving town today.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
So I have to go there alone? In the slum?

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Don't worry. I already told them about you, and they are expecting you.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
This is not what I'm worried about.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Oh, I see.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Look, the slum just has a bad reputation. Not all the people living in the slum are criminals, and most are too poor to live elsewhere.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
I suppose you are right.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
Yeah. And if anyone tries to bother you, just tell them you are here to meet Mr. Victor.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
No one will dare to do anything after that.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Alright. I'll remember that.

\c[2]Tomas:\c[0]
So anyway, I should go. I have a bus to catch.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Okay, bye.
Quest Add 10
